subject: [PATCH v5 9/9] mm: switch deferred split shrinker to list_lru

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change improves handling of cgroups in a suboptimal fashion by avoiding imperfect guard rails and streamlining list operations and reclaim walks. The update also adds calls to create node heads for cgroups on demand when the first object of interest is allocated, eliminating the need for special care in migration code between nodes.

reviews.author: SeongJae Park
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is split across multiple nodes, and requested that the list_lru per-memcg heads be instantiated on demand when the first object of interest is allocated for a cgroup.

Reviewer SeongJae Park reported that the patch causes a build failure in UM mode due to an implicit function declaration error, specifically for the folio_memcg_alloc_deferred() function when CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE is unset. They suggested defining the function for this case and provided a temporal fix as evidence.

Reviewer SeongJae Park noted that the patch is missing a definition for folio_memcg_alloc_deferred() when CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPPAGE is unset, causing an implicit function declaration error in UM mode kunit tests.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Johannes,

On Wed, 27 May 2026 16:45:16 -0400 Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org> wrote:

---

I found this patch is now in mm-new and it makes UM mode kunit fails like
below.

    $ ./tools/testing/kunit/kunit.py run --kunitconfig mm/damon/tests/
    [00:00:02] Configuring KUnit Kernel ...
    [00:00:02] Building KUnit Kernel ...
    Populating config with:
    $ make ARCH=um O=.kunit olddefconfig
    Building with:
    $ make all compile_commands.json scripts_gdb ARCH=um O=.kunit --jobs=8
    ERROR:root:../mm/swap_state.c: In function \u2018__swap_cache_alloc\u2019:
    ../mm/swap_state.c:468:26: error: implicit declaration of function \u2018folio_memcg_alloc_deferred\u2019 [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
      468 |         if (order > 1 && folio_memcg_alloc_deferred(folio)) {
          |                          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    make[4]: *** [../scripts/Makefile.build:289: mm/swap_state.o] Error 1
    make[4]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
    make[3]: *** [../scripts/Makefile.build:548: mm] Error 2
    make[3]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
    make[2]: *** [/home/lkhack/linux/Makefile:2143: .] Error 2
    make[1]: *** [/home/lkhack/linux/Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
    make: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2

Maybe we can define the function for CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPAGE unset case?  I
confirmed the below attaching temporal fix works for at least kunit.


Thanks,
SJ

[...]
=== >8 ===

---

From: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>
Date: Wed, 27 May 2026 23:58:07 -0700
Subject: [PATCH] mm/huge_mm: define memcg_alloc_deferred() for
 !CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPPAGE
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=UTF-8
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit

Without this, UM mode kunit fails like below.

    $ ./tools/testing/kunit/kunit.py run --kunitconfig mm/damon/tests/
    [00:00:02] Configuring KUnit Kernel ...
    [00:00:02] Building KUnit Kernel ...
    Populating config with:
    $ make ARCH=um O=.kunit olddefconfig
    Building with:
    $ make all compile_commands.json scripts_gdb ARCH=um O=.kunit --jobs=8
    ERROR:root:../mm/swap_state.c: In function \u2018__swap_cache_alloc\u2019:
    ../mm/swap_state.c:468:26: error: implicit declaration of function \u2018folio_memcg_alloc_deferred\u2019 [-Wimplicit-function-declaration]
      468 |         if (order > 1 && folio_memcg_alloc_deferred(folio)) {
          |                          ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
    make[4]: *** [../scripts/Makefile.build:289: mm/swap_state.o] Error 1
    make[4]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
    make[3]: *** [../scripts/Makefile.build:548: mm] Error 2
    make[3]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
    make[2]: *** [/home/lkhack/linux/Makefile:2143: .] Error 2
    make[1]: *** [/home/lkhack/linux/Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2
    make: *** [Makefile:248: __sub-make] Error 2

Fix by implementing the function for CONFIG_TRANSPARENT_HUGEPPAGE unset
case.

Fixes: https://lore.kernel.org/20260527204757.2544958-10-hannes@cmpxchg.org
Signed-off-by: SeongJae Park <sj@kernel.org>
---
 include/linux/huge_mm.h | 5 +++++
 1 file changed, 5 insertions(+)

diff --git a/include/linux/huge_mm.h b/include/linux/huge_mm.h
index f6c2531a27a35..055de7b8ed487 100644
--- a/include/linux/huge_mm.h
+++ b/include/linux/huge_mm.h
@@ -663,6 +663,11 @@ static inline int folio_split(struct folio *folio, unsigned int new_order,
 	return -EINVAL;
 }
 
+static inline int folio_memcg_alloc_deferred(struct folio *folio)
+{
+	return 0;
+}
+
 static inline void deferred_split_folio(struct folio *folio, bool partially_mapped) {}
 #define split_huge_pmd(__vma, __pmd, __address)	\
 	do { } while (0)
-- 
2.47.3

reviews.author: Usama Arif
reviews.summary: Reviewer questioned the necessity of incrementing count_mthp_stat(order, MTHP_STAT_ANON_FAULT_FALLBACK) in a specific scenario, suggesting either removing it or modifying the control flow to avoid this increment
reviews.raw_body:
Ah sorry, should have caught this in the previous version, do we need

count_mthp_stat(order, MTHP_STAT_ANON_FAULT_FALLBACK);

here?

or maybe we just goto next instead of goto fallback and trty next
viable order?

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author addressed Usama Arif's concern that an unexpected failure of a sub-costly-order slab allocation could be missed by the event counter, explaining that such failures are rare and would already be logged in dmesg.

Author addressed a concern about the naming convention for the list_lru allocation, agreeing that 'fallback' is more correct and choosing to leave the code as is.
reviews.raw_body:
This isn't an allocation we expect to fail with any sort of routine
that we'd need to capture it in the event counter. It would warn in
dmesg if it did. But in practice it can't happen at all, since it's a
sub-costly-order slab allocation and the allocator would loop and OOM
kill stuff until it succeeds.

---

Again I don't think it matters, but fallback seems a bit more correct
because the size of the list_lru allocation doesn't change with lower
orders (until we hit 0).

So I think we can just leave it as is.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a mistake in their testing and promised to add UM (User Mode) builds to their final compile test before resubmitting the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Whoops, thanks for the fix, SJ. I'll incorporate UM builds into my
final compile test before sending.

Acked-by: Johannes Weiner <hannes@cmpxchg.org>

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change addresses a suboptimal handling of cgroups in the deferred split queue, where large pages on other nodes were being split unnecessarily. The new approach streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and is used by other major shrinkers already. The patch also adds calls to create node heads for the cgroup at once when splittable pages are created, eliminating the need for special care in migration code between nodes.

reviews.author: Kairui Song
reviews.summary: Reviewer suggested moving error handling under a shared label for consistency and potential future simplification
reviews.raw_body:
Thanks!

Nit: I think it would be better if we move the error handling under a
label to be shared with the charge failure above, but it's fine this
way, can be simplified later.

Reviewed-by: Kairui Song <kasong@tencent.com>

This change addresses a suboptimal handling of cgroups in the deferred split queue, where large pages on other nodes were being split unnecessarily. The new approach streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and is used by other major shrinkers already. The patch also adds calls to create node heads for the cgroup at once when splittable pages are created, eliminating the need for special care in migration code between nodes.

reviews.author: Wei Yang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Wei Yang pointed out that there are three possible failure points and some duplicate count_xxx_event/state() calls in the patch, suggesting a follow-up cleanup to address these issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Nit: we have three possible failure point, and some duplicate
count_xxx_event/state().

Maybe we can have a followup cleanup for it.

Others, looks good. Thanks.

-- 
Wei Yang
Help you, Help me

2026-05-31.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change addresses a suboptimal handling of cgroups in the deferred split queue, where large pages on other nodes were being split unnecessarily. The new approach streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and is used by other major shrinkers already. The patch also adds calls to create node heads for the cgroup at once when splittable pages are created, eliminating the need for special care in migration code between nodes.

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lance Yang noted that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is already on the list_lru when it's split, and requested that the code be updated to properly remove the folio from the list before splitting.

The reviewer noted that the patch does not include an MTHP_STAT_ANON_FAULT_FALLBACK increment, which is necessary because the deferred split queue bypasses the normal page fault handling and jumps directly to fallback, resulting in order-0 pages being created.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is split across multiple nodes and its corresponding memcg has already been freed, leading to a use-after-free bug.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, May 27, 2026 at 04:45:16PM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:
[...]

---

Missing a MTHP_STAT_ANON_FAULT_FALLBACK bump here?

Since we jump straight to fallback and end up at order-0 :)

---

[...]

Cheers, Lance

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary: Reviewer Lance Yang initially raised an issue but later withdrew it by stating 'Never mind this one, please.'
reviews.raw_body:
Sorry for the noise, I missed the earlier discussion. Never mind this 
one, please.

reviews.author: Lance Yang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lance Yang noted that the patch does not handle the case where a folio is split across multiple nodes and its memcg has been migrated to another node, which can lead to incorrect deferred split queue updates.

Reviewer Lance Yang suggested limiting the deferred split shrinker switch to anonymous swap-in paths, citing a specific condition (vmf && vma_is_anonymous(vmf->vma)) and implying that this would be sufficient for the change.

The reviewer pointed out that the patch does not filter shmem swap-in operations from using folio_memcg_alloc_deferred(), which allocates memcg list_lru metadata for deferred_split_lru, but only queues anon large folios. This means that shmem swap-in can also trigger the allocation of this metadata, even though it doesn't benefit from it.
reviews.raw_body:
On Wed, May 27, 2026 at 04:45:16PM -0400, Johannes Weiner wrote:
[...]

---

Shouldn't this be limited to anon swapin?

e.g. vmf && vma_is_anonymous(vmf->vma)

---

__swap_cache_alloc() is also used by shmem direct swapin, so shmem can
get here too when handling a large swap entry:

shmem_get_folio_gfp()
  shmem_swapin_folio()
    shmem_swap_alloc_folio()
      swapin_sync()
        swap_cache_alloc_folio()
          __swap_cache_alloc()
            folio_memcg_alloc_deferred()

@Baolin please correct me if I got it wrong :)

folio_memcg_alloc_deferred() itself doesn't filter shmem out either; it
only allocates the memcg list_lru metadata for deferred_split_lru:

int folio_memcg_alloc_deferred(struct folio *folio)
{
	if (mem_cgroup_disabled())
		return 0;
	return folio_memcg_list_lru_alloc(folio, &deferred_split_lru, GFP_KERNEL);
}

Since deferred_split_lru only queues anon large folios, doing this for
shmem swapin doesn't buy us anything :)

Maybe I'm missing something, just wanted to raise it.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary:
Author acknowledged a mistake in dismissing the branch for direct swapin, but no specific fix is planned.

Author acknowledged that the deferred split queue needs to be fixed and agreed to send a follow-up patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Good catch, I think you're right. I shouldn't have dismissed that
branch due to "/* Direct swapin skipping swap cache & readahead */"

---

Yes, agreed. I don't think it's a big deal / show stopper in terms of
user-visible effect, but of course still worth fixing.

I'll send a follow-up patch.

2026-06-01.patch_summary: This patch updates the deferred split shrinker to use list_lru, which properly sets up per-node and per-cgroup lists. This change addresses a suboptimal handling of cgroups in the deferred split queue, where large pages on other nodes were being split unnecessarily. The new approach streamlines list operations and reclaim walks, and is used by other major shrinkers already. The patch also adds calls to create node heads for the cgroup at once when splittable pages are created, eliminating the need for special care in migration code between nodes.
